reason: The statement applies because Nehemiah, the protagonist in this text fragment, was a prophet who was associated with the enemies of the Hebrews. He was serving as the governor of Judah under the Persian king Artaxerxes, who would have been considered a foreign ruler by the Hebrews. Additionally, Nehemiah interacted with non-Hebrew rulers and officials throughout the text, indicating his association with the enemies of the Hebrews.
